current.name: MWO Amos Kassem
current.bio:
Warrant Officer (Class One) Amos Kassem was born in 1973 to the family of Mr and Mrs Lupwi K. Chingyin in Lur District of Kanke Local Government Area of Plateau State. He was enlisted into the Nigerian Army in 1993 as a member of 36 Regular Intake.

Throughout his military career, WO1 Kassem has served with commitment, professionalism and loyalty, contributing to the maintenance of peace, security and the defence of Nigeria's territorial integrity. His experience spans several years of active service during which he has participated in military operations, training exercises and internal security operations. His leadership qualities, commitment to regimentation and dedication to mentoring young soldiers have earned him the respect of colleagues and superiors alike.

He has attended various military courses including: Counter-Terrorist Course (Jaji), Amphibious Course (Calabar), Quick Response Course (Jaji), Regimental Intelligence Course TEGO (Lagos), Platoon Weapon Course (Jaji), Security Management (Bajams, Zaria), and Warrant Officer Academy Course (Jaji).

He holds a National Diploma in Security Management from Nuhu Bamalli Polytechnic, Zaria.

He has served in the following operations: ECOMOG, Operation Harmony IV Bakassi, African Mission in Sudan, Operation Hadin Kai (Maiduguri), Operation Polo Shield (Port Harcourt), and ECOMIG 4.

WO1 Kassem is happily married and blessed with three children.
